summary: Completed full E2E testing of Geo-Energy Intelligence platform. Backend API 9/9 tests pass. Frontend UI loads correctly, all interactions work. Minor CSS issue with risk badge colors for certain risk levels.

ui_bugs.issue: Risk badges for 'low' and 'medium' risk levels (Vegetation NDVI, Terrain Slope) render with no visible color/text - appear as white/empty boxes. Tailwind arbitrary color classes `text-geo-DEFAULT` and `text-solar-DEFAULT` may not be resolved at runtime. Only 'high' risk badge (red) renders correctly.

issues: 'Solar Irradiation' label text slightly cut off when sidebar width is tight - minor truncation

action_items: Fix RiskBadge component CSS: `text-geo-DEFAULT` and `text-solar-DEFAULT` Tailwind classes are not rendering correctly (likely not in safelist). Use explicit color values or ensure custom colors are in tailwind.config.js safelist.
Verify Tailwind custom color aliases (geo-DEFAULT, solar-DEFAULT, wind-DEFAULT, navy-*) are properly configured in tailwind.config.js
critical_code_review_comments: RiskBadge in Sidebar.tsx uses Tailwind custom color classes bg-geo-DEFAULT/15, text-geo-DEFAULT, bg-solar-DEFAULT/15 — these opacity-modified custom colors may not work without proper Tailwind safelist configuration
server.py has no MongoDB dependency (pure in-memory computation) - this is by design for synthetic data
No authentication or rate limiting on /api/analyze-region endpoint - acceptable for MVP
seed_data_creation: None required - synthetic data computed per request
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a Next.js + React-Leaflet GIS platform. Map draw functionality works via mousedown/mousemove/mouseup on Leaflet canvas. Must zoom in (5+ clicks on + button) before drawing for area to be within 800,000 km² limit. Backend uses pure synthetic GIS calculations with no database. All 4 layer toggles functional, energy tabs functional, analysis pipeline works end-to-end.
rca_of_issue: RiskBadge CSS issue: Tailwind JIT compiler purges unused classes. Custom color aliases like `geo-DEFAULT`, `solar-DEFAULT` used in template literals with opacity modifier `/15` may not be detected by Tailwind's content scanner. Fix: add these classes to tailwind.config.js safelist array or use inline styles.
